main.description: The wire atom: a JSON wrapper around a typed lexicon record. The wrapper core (id, sender, kind, epoch) is frozen and epoch-protected; record is the free-evolution zone. Messages travel enveloped; artifacts and registry records are stored bare. See ADR-0006, ADR-0010.

id.description: A ULID set by the sender; also the dedup key. Its embedded timestamp is sender-set, so it is not a trusted clock (the bus stamp is).

sender.description: The authenticated identity that published the message — the credential's JWT name. Set from auth, never an input; cannot be forged.

kind.description: The frame type. 'message' is the only frame today; other frames may appear in a later epoch.

epoch.description: The protocol epoch this envelope was written under. Checked per-message, because durable streams outlive epochs.

record.description: The typed lexicon record (a $type-tagged object). Opaque to the bus; always JSON on this wire. Binary is inline base64 today, native under a later DAG-CBOR encoding, or a blob reference when large (ADR-0016).
